name: OPEC+
summary: Day 87 of Hormuz blockade (Monday May 25 — US Memorial Day; ICE Brent traded LIVE/thin, WTI FROZEN). **BRENT BREAKS $100 (~$100.21 LIVE, intraday sub-$100 ~$98, -3.2%); WTI ~$91 LIVE — DEAL-ANTICIPATION RE-PRICING IS NOW THE STRUCTURAL TEST FOR OPEC+ ARITHMETIC. US-Iran deal progress (Doha delegation working Hormuz + frozen-asset mechanics) dumped the Hormuz tail-risk premium. If Brent closes sub-$100 for 3 sessions, Goldman's $120 Q3 base case is mathematically foreclosed and JPM's $60 2026-average case re-enters the band — a direct re-pricing of the price floor OPEC+ supply policy is managing against. The brent-crude→opec-plus edge fired today on this deal-anticipation re-price. STALENESS CORRECTION: the prior May 9 lead carried Brent ~$101.73 and Goldman Q2 $90 — both now superseded; the structural arithmetic has shifted from 'how high does the Hormuz premium run' to 'does the deal de-price the premium toward the OPEC+ floor.' Critical contradiction in the deal framework: if Iran reopens Hormuz and US lifts sanctions, eventual Iranian crude return to the market is a MEDIUM-TERM bearish factor stacked on top of the UAE OPEC exit (effective May 1, removes ~3M+ bpd theoretical spare from cartel arithmetic) and forecast cuts. June 7 OPEC+ ministerial remains the structural test for substantive Saudi response post-UAE-exit; with UAE out, the architecture rests on Saudi-Russia-Iraq-Kuwait. Saudi break-even ~$95/bbl is now ABOVE the live Brent print — fiscal-runway pressure on the war's primary fiscal beneficiary intensifies as Brent breaks $100.** PRIOR (May 9): Day 71 Saturday. **OPEC+ PRODUCTION CUT MAINTENANCE CONTINUES THROUGH MAY — ~$2-3 CONTRIBUTION TO CURRENT BRENT FLOOR INDEPENDENT OF HORMUZ.** Brent close $101.73 May 8 (+1.66% 1D, intraday range $96-104 = trinary-regime signature). Goldman Q2 $90 target now stretched against Lloyd's List zero-transit print May 7 + 1,500-vessel Persian Gulf standstill + CENTCOM 70-tanker blockade scale + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' Hormuz speech — likely revises higher if trinary regime extends past May 21. World Bank $86 average for 2026 still operative in baseline. Saudi Tadawul +0.7% May 8 (treated cautiously — Friday partial trading; Sunday open will be first reaction to Saudi Al-Hadath denial of US offensive-airspace lift). UAE OPEC exit effective May 1; UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity medium-term-BEARISH (12-24mo) for Brent (Atlantic Council). WoodMac: ADNOC Murban discount potentially widening to $3.50/bbl by Q3. **June 7 OPEC+ ministerial remains the structural test** for substantive Saudi response post-UAE-exit. With UAE OUT, post-OPEC architecture rests on Saudi-Russia-Iraq-Kuwait. **Critical contradiction in MOU framework:** if Iran ships out HEU and US lifts sanctions per MOU provisions, eventual Iranian crude return to global market adds medium-term bearish factor on top of UAE exit + forecast cuts. PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): MBS Day 9+ silence on UAE OPEC exit; Tadawul peace-dividend bearish signal for war's primary fiscal beneficiary; Saudi break-even ~$95/bbl threatens fiscal runway.

current.saudi_position: carrying more burden post-UAE; MBS publicly silent; needs higher revenue post-Sadara damage

active_triggers.condition: OPEC+ June ministerial announces production unwind

recent_signals.headline: **BRENT BREAKS $100 (~$100.21 LIVE, INTRADAY SUB-$100, -3.2%); WTI ~$91 LIVE — DEAL-ANTICIPATION RE-PRICING IS NOW THE STRUCTURAL TEST FOR OPEC+ ARITHMETIC.** (1) **Mechanism**: US-Iran deal progress (senior Iranian delegation in Doha working Hormuz + frozen-asset mechanics) dumped the Hormuz tail-risk premium in the LIVE clean-room session (US Memorial Day — WTI/NYMEX frozen). If Brent closes sub-$100 for 3 sessions, Goldman's $120 Q3 base case is mathematically foreclosed and JPM's $60 2026-average case re-enters the band — a direct re-pricing of the price floor OPEC+ supply policy is managing against. (2) **Critical contradiction in the deal framework**: if Iran reopens Hormuz and the US lifts sanctions, eventual Iranian crude return to the global market is a MEDIUM-TERM bearish factor stacked on the UAE OPEC exit (effective May 1, removes ~3M+ bpd theoretical spare from cartel arithmetic — EIA cut OPEC 2027 spare-capacity forecast to 2.5M b/d from 3.8M) and forecast cuts. (3) **Saudi fiscal pressure**: break-even ~$95/bbl is now ABOVE the live Brent print — pressure on the war's primary fiscal beneficiary intensifies as Brent breaks $100. (4) **June 7 OPEC+ ministerial** remains the structural test for substantive Saudi response post-UAE-exit; with UAE out, the architecture rests on Saudi-Russia-Iraq-Kuwait. (Staleness correction: this node had not updated since May 9 — the prior Brent ~$101.73 / Goldman Q2 $90 framing is superseded.)
sources: ICE Brent LIVE ~$100.21 / -3.2% (markets dossier 2026-05-25 C1; thin holiday tape, WTI FROZEN)
Euronews / Daily Sabah / CNN May 25 (Iranian delegation in Doha — Hormuz + frozen-asset mechanics; intel A3)
Markets dossier 2026-05-25 C1 (Goldman $120 Q3 foreclosed / JPM $60 re-enters if Brent closes sub-$100 3 sessions)
Carried: UAE OPEC exit effective May 1 (removes ~3M+ bpd theoretical spare; EIA 2027 spare-capacity 2.5M b/d)
recent_signals.verification: Brent ~$100.21 / -3.2% LIVE = CONFIRMED MARKET DATA (thin holiday session); WTI ~$91 LIVE vs frozen yfinance = CONFIRMED data-status caveat (US Memorial Day); deal-anticipation re-pricing = CONFIRMED PROCESS (US-Iran progress on record, deal NOT signed); Iranian crude-return bearish-stack = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS; UAE OPEC exit = CONFIRMED (effective May 1); June 7 ministerial = CONFIRMED scheduled.
